name: Summer Circus Spectacular
venue: Historic Asolo Theater at The Ringling
hours_label: Tue–Fri 11 AM & 2 PM, Sat 2 & 5 PM · $20 adult / $15 child
blurb: The Circus Arts Conservatory and The Ringling team up for Sarasota's beloved summer tradition, with world-class circus artists performing in the air-conditioned, jewel-box Historic Asolo Theater. Tickets are $20 for adults and $15 for kids 12 and under, and adding the Circus Museum is just $5 more.

name: The Last Romance
dates_label: Opens Wed, Jul 22 — runs through Aug 16
blurb: Florida Studio Theatre opens its summer mainstage comedy about Ralph, a widower whose chance meeting with the elegant Carol turns into an unexpected second chance at love. Warm, witty and about 1 hour 50 minutes with intermission. Tickets through the FST box office at 941-366-9000 — a good excuse to make a night of it downtown.

name: Sarasota Farmers Market
address: Lemon Ave & Main St, Sarasota, FL 34236
blurb: Downtown Sarasota's Saturday-morning staple: fresh produce, flowers, baked goods, coffee and local makers along Lemon Avenue and Main Street. Free to browse, dog-friendly, and the easiest way to see downtown at its liveliest — go early to beat the July heat.

name: Siesta Key Drum Circle
blurb: Siesta Key's famous Sunday-evening ritual since 1996: drummers, dancers and hundreds of onlookers gather on the quartz sand south of the pavilion as the sun drops into the Gulf. Free, all ages, and quintessentially Siesta — arrive early for parking.
